ActionItems: Wait for Iverson to confirm property tax situation #1
BreakEvenAnalysis.Context: Quick back-of-napkin after Sensei Iverson mentioned property taxes might bump lease cost. Pre-Allison review. #1

CurrentState.Note: Rough estimate only. Need to revisit with Allison once 2025 actuals are closed. #1

BreakEvenAnalysis.KeyInsight: Break-even is ~28-30 members at current cost structure. At 55 members we have plenty of headroom. Main concern was seasonal dip but even at 48 (Dec low) we're safe. #1

Scenario_A_RentTo750.BreakEvenCalculation: Fixed costs $2,670 + $100 increase = $2,770. At $95/member, $2,770 / $95 = 29.2 → 30 members minimum. #1
Scenario_A_RentTo750.Impact: Minimal. Well within buffer at 55 members. #1
Scenario_A_RentTo750.Label: Rent increases to $750/mo if property tax passes through #1

Scenario_B_RentTo850.Impact: Still fine. Iverson said this was unlikely but wanted to stress test. #1
Scenario_B_RentTo850.Label: Worst case - rent to $850/mo (unlikely per Iverson) #1
